My main goal for everyone just to see you guys have some metrics to tie this to is to get everyone to re-sign at least 75% of prospects into members. So if you have goals for your facilities, that's what I would make as a goal. Can you sign higher? Like they are? Yes, absolutely. But for me, the goal on the back end of any front end trial should be three out of four. For me, I would like 50% of the customers to leave five star reviews, so that's on an ongoing basis. We're going to actually have some automated campaigns around that which Nick briefly touched on from Skippyo. I want 25% of those people to upsell into HTTP. Ryan, what percent of you upsell into HTTP off the back end in six weeks? Is it 20? Is it lower? I can change that. It's higher. Okay, so you're doing a third. I might lower it to like 20%, but basically you want to still have an active, like a predictable amount of people who are upselling into a higher ticket back end offer. Yeah, so those are the things that I wanted to talk about, those are like the kind of, and have less than 10% back out after signing up. So that's kind of like the closer formula, like the R of reinforce everything and sell them again on the fact how amazing everything is and continue to sell them. That's what you need to be doing. Okay, so are you guys doing anything different on touch points during the six week challenge? Rather than like weekly reach outs with text messages, or anything that you guys are doing that, because I would like to know why they're closing 95% too. We didn't do that. So are you doing anything that, like in those touch points that you're doing week to week, is there anything particular that you've done that's different or outstanding? Who starts the abandoned hope sale by week three of their challenge? All right, who starts it by week two? Week one? Who starts it in the first time they ever meet the customer? Cool, so everybody should be up at this point. Because the sale doesn't happen miraculously out of nowhere as a surprise on week three. You have to plant seeds from the very first minute that they walk in and explain to them that what they're doing here is not a six week magic bullet. In fact, I think it's even in the ad copy. They need to understand that this entire process is about showing them a healthy lifestyle where they're going to be able to sustain it and stay with you. In my interviews, the initial interviews I explained to them, and we're not shy about it, we say, look, the reason we offer this for free is because we're so confident in the product that we're going to provide you. We have three, well I don't tell them three, but I tell them we have six weeks to impress you enough to earn your business for life. And that is our goal. So that is why we're so confident to put our foot forward and offer you a potential to earn this for free, because we know you're not going anywhere. So you have to plant seeds from day one, make them understand that they're going to stay. Plant seeds with your trainers every single time they're in the class. Talk to them about their progress three months from now, six months from now. Talk about events and parties you're going to have. We talk about our Halloween party in October and how much fun it was last year, and we post pictures of it. Paint a picture for these people to understand that they're not going anywhere. So that's the first thing, and again, I don't know if this is something that we're doing different from what you had, but the sale starts from the first interaction and it never stops. By the time you're getting into that week three weigh-in, you're not selling anything. You're just reiterating what's already been done throughout the entire process. And they should have already made that decision from day one, and you're just enforced that every single time they were in the gym. Make sense? Yeah, no, totally. Monica, you do anything special here? Oh, sorry. I was leaning behind her and we all sketched you like, hey, let's talk to them about it. Oh, it is. Good. There you go. Yeah. So with the back end, basically, it's my partner, Sukhwi. She's the one who's closing. So going back to what Trevor actually just spoke about, which is funny, because I've been in sales for about 20 years and very high ticket. So for me, this is actually like one of the lowest price products I've ever sold. But then again, it's also a different demographic. This is a demographic that may be more price sensitive mentally, because it's essentially not a luxury targeted audience. It's just general folks, right? So do you guys ever run into like pricing objections or someone, oh, I didn't realize that gym is this expensive? Okay. So the problem there is that you're selling a number. You're selling a price. And there is no such thing as overpriced. Everything on the planet is overpriced. You can't have things that are overvalued, but they're not overpriced. So it's up to you to establish a value. I'll give you a perfect example. Would anyone give me five grand for this microphone? No? Okay. Would anyone give me five grand if I sent Alex down to your gym to sell for a full week? Okay. So I didn't change a number, right? 5,000 is still 5,000. What I did is I attached the value to that number. So if you're hearing from your customer, oh, it's too expensive or I can't afford it, what they're really telling you is you haven't shown me why that value is big enough. Okay. So you have to attack the value objection before it even comes up because if they're thinking numbers, you just haven't sold them on the product. So you have three weeks leading into that point to really wow them and make them understand that they're buying physical, social. They're essentially buying, I know you said this in the video, they're buying a new identity, right? They're buying a new family, they're buying a new lifestyle and that has tremendous value. You have to reinforce the value of what you're giving them and it's far more than losing 20 pounds. So if the number comes up, you just haven't attached the proper value to it. And again, that doesn't start in that 15 minute weigh-in at week three. That starts from the first interview. And if you make them believe you like Trevor just said, if they believe that what you're really giving them is genuine and you care, they just can't leave. I mean, unless they're psychotic, there's no way they're walking out the door. It doesn't make sense. Yes. So I want to get this type of tag. So you do 101. So I wish I could give you a really sexy answer and I always want everyone to improve off of what I had. So I want you to have better gyms, more profitable gyms than I had. I did Abandoned Hope 101. So why don't we go from the moments when we walk in the door all the way to the time they sign their paper with you? I schedule them. I usually do two hours in the morning, two hours in the evening, 15 minutes apart. So I'll get eight people in. Normally, we did only two launches with this program, although we've been doing challenges for a little over a year now. And we've had about between 1560 sign-ups for the two launches. So it takes me about a week and a half to get through all of the participants. We do emphasize that it's mandatory. It's in our initial agreement. It's one of the things they do agree to in the initial when they sign up. So the midway weigh-ins are mandatory. And if they miss it, they essentially are disqualified from their potential for a refund. When they walk in, you're going to have one or two things. You're going to either have someone who's extremely excited because they just lost a bunch of weight or body fat and they're wowed, or you're going to have someone that's slightly disappointed. I think the latter is more common because even if we know as a professional that they're doing well, in their mind, if they didn't lose 75 pounds in the first six days, they failed. Especially women. It's like sometimes they're down three pounds on the scale and 4% body fat, but they're so obsessed with that three-pound loss that they're just coming in all depressed. So the number one thing is, and again, not everyone has this luxury, but I'm fortunate to have my partner Sal kind of greet them at the door, do their way, and I'm upstairs in the office. I have a little walkie talkies with earpieces and he'll tell me, you know, Samantha's here, I'm weighing her now. I said, okay, no problem. Let me know when she's done. And then he'll tell me she's ready. I'll say, go ahead, send her up. And from the time she's walking up, he'll say, listen, she's pissed off. I said, okay. Awesome. Cool. So she'll come in and I have a big smile on my face. When she walks in the door, hey, Sam, how you been? All right. How are you feeling? Oh, I feel good, but I'm flopping this. I suck. I said, why? Let me see. I said, what's wrong? You're freaking crushing it. Really? Yeah, you're doing awesome. Give me a high five. She's confused, but she's like, okay. So then I explained it to her. I said, look, you know, we're doing a lot of resistance training. You're putting on some muscle. Your body's adjusting. Look, think of how sore you were after the first class. Here we are only three weeks later. We've only had 12 classes. Did you miss any? Yeah, I missed two. So you've been here 10 hours. All right. That's one work day. Think of the progress you made in one work day. We were in here doing 100 squats yesterday and here you are walking up the stairs. You couldn't get out of bed two weeks ago. Big win. Give me another high five. Just get them excited. People are not going to remember 20 minutes after this what you say, but they're going to remember how you make them feel. Anytime you're selling something, you're selling an emotion while you're painting a picture. So it's not what you say, it's in the articulation of how you say it and the emotional buttons that you're able to push. The more emotional buttons you're able to generate out of someone, the more likely they're going to have a response that you want. And it's up to you to lead them to where you want them to go. If any of you guys remember any instances that happened to you back in high school, you may not remember specific conversations. You may not even remember a conversation you had at breakfast today, but you remember very vividly conversations or instances that happened 20 years ago. And that's because they left an emotional scar or an emotional high. And if you can generate an emotion out of people and it's a positive one, they're going to react in a positive way. So if you can make that person feel like, eh, you care, be there doing great, then it's really up to you to just put out a new blueprint. And then you can get a laugh out of them. And I'm like, it's up to you? I mean, your call. Everybody else is doing it. That's because that's how we've always done it. So we just hit it. Any lap? What happens if someone's already crushing in the challenge? Like, how do you get them to take the bite? So those that are likely to hit their goals are easy because they're doing well. You don't even have to overcome that initial emotion of turning them around. So here's the deal. Just because you started, what's the number one thing everyone complains of when they start the gym? Not necessarily with you guys when they go to the gym. Oh, the first couple of weeks, I'm crushing it, and then I plateau, right? So you've got to explain to them, look, progress does slow down. You can't, you're not going to lose 6% body fat every six weeks for the rest of your life. It's not going to happen. So just because it slows down doesn't mean that you're going to stop. It means that that's the time for us to tweak and get you kickstarted again. Now you made a U-turn because what happened in the six weeks before you started, you were probably gaining weight. You made a U-turn the day you walked in here. Now you go in the right direction. So the only thing missing right now is more time under your belt. If I need to get, and we're in New York, so I always use Florida as an analogy, if I say if I want to go to Florida, I can walk, I could drive, or I could fly. They're all going to get me there just at different times. So if I'm heading the right direction, that's the key. So now you're going the right direction. All we need is more time under our belt. So the fact that you're at the midway point and you're doing well, A, does not guarantee that you'll get there before the expiration. So if you want to roll the dice, it's on you. And B, don't forget, I'm not just giving you a refund today and essentially guaranteeing your success. You've effectively won your challenge. I'm giving you an additional three weeks. And in this case, we end February 11 from this run. So it ends up being three weeks, maybe two weeks. But you're incentivizing it further. This is something on top of that credit that they do not have an opportunity to earn if they get up from this table today. So there is more to saying yes now than there is to saying yes at week six. That help? Sweet. She was saying about U-turn. Oh, you want to explain the three-legged stool? I think you did own one of the Zoom calls, and I think it was really valuable. Yeah, that's something that I do in the initial interview when people come in. And I tell them it's three pillars to success. I look at it as a tripod. And then your goals are right up on top of the tripod. So I ask the group, because I do those in groups of 12 or 15, I'll say, so if I have a tripod, which one of the three legs is most important, A, B, or C? And some people think it's a trick question, and they'll actually give me a letter. But most people will be like, well, they're all equally important, because if you take any of them away, the whole thing collapses. So the three pillars of your goal are accountability, proper fitness, and proper nutrition. Where have you gone in the past that had all three of those? Nowhere. That's why you've always bounced around different programs and different gyms, and that's why you've never hit your goals. We're here to give you all three of those legs. And guess what? It's not just for six weeks. It's for life. None of those ever goes away. You're always going to have the accountability of your group and your coaches. You're always going to have the fitness aspect. You're always going to have the nutritional aspect. That's why our program's successful, and that's why you're not going anywhere in three weeks or in six weeks. And if you want to start doing members for life, it's like an easy add-on to even increase the conviction. You said you wanted to lose weight for life. All of them were for life. So you actually want to be done with this problem. That's the type of facility that we are. Whoa, and then you start building up Gucci again. But then you get the tried on my own. At the halfway, or at the end, if you're trying to save them, well, you know what I'm thinking about doing on my own? It's like, remember the tripod? So your whole life, you've failed this entire time, and you finally had the tripod, and you hit your goal. And so right now, you want to take away one of the legs? Really? And then they're like, I guess you're, you know what I mean? They just look, they have to basically admit, I'm an idiot, and then I'll wait. They have to make that kind of commitment.
